title: NOC 21120 – Public & Environmental Health and Safety Professionals | GTR Immigration
description: Public and Environmental Health and Safety Professionals play a critical role in protecting communities, workplaces, and ecosystems across Canada. Classified under NOC 21120 (TEER 1), this occupation supports multiple skilled-worker immigration pathways, including Express Entry–aligned streams, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and employer-driven work permits.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree
classification.minor_group: 2112 – Public and environmental health and safety professionals

duties: Inspecting restaurants, hospitals, schools, and public facilities
Monitoring natural environments for pollution sources
Investigating hazardous spills or disease outbreaks
Enforcing municipal, provincial, or federal regulations
Delivering training to workers and employers
Advising communities on health and safety practices
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in environmental studies, food science, chemistry, or health & safety
Certification with the Canadian Institute of Public Health Inspectors (outside Quebec)
BCRSP certification for safety professionals may be required
job_outlook: From 2022–2031, Canada is expected to generate 7,900 new positions, with around 6,600 job seekers—indicating favourable immigration prospects for internationally trained professionals.
additional_information: Demand for public and environmental health and safety specialists continues to rise nationwide, driven by regulatory compliance, climate resilience planning, and workplace-safety standards.

Municipal, provincial, and federal governmentsEnvironmental consulting firmsHealthcare and hospitality facilitiesManufacturing and industrial operationsUtilities and waste-management companiesEducational institutions
Annual earnings usually range between $42,400–$109,700 CAD, depending on province, certification, and seniority.
